title: fraxinus gooddingii
intro: Fraxinus gooddingii, also known as Goodding's ash, is a deciduous tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the olive family (Oleaceae). The bark and leaves of Fraxinus gooddingii contain saponins, which are compounds with known medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Fraxinus gooddingii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antiviral properties. Fraxinus gooddingii can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
